{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=draft|12=draft}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft|12=draft}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|ja|土岐氏|12=draft|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=draft|12=draft}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Japan)|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō|ja|左義長|12=draft|12=draft}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.comment: 90 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市) up to rev 104132438
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=手力雄神社 | 画像=[[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
いちょう通りから見た様子 | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[各務原市]][[那加手力町]]4 | 位置={{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]]
[[金幣社]] | 創建=[[6世紀]] | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[4月28日]]・[[4月29日|29日]] | 神事= | 画像説明=いちょう通りから見た様子 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=手力雄神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''手力雄神社'''（てぢからおおじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[各務原市]]那加にある[[神社]]。各務原市[[那加町|那加地区]]の[[産土神]]（那加総社）で、各務原市内[[金幣社|金幣五社]]の一社である。 == 歴史 == [[6世紀]]末期ごろ中里（現那加地区）を支配していた豪族により、佐良木郷八ケ村（西市場、新加納、前野、北洞、岩地、長塚、桐野、山後）の山の中腹に[[磐座]]を祀ったのが始まり。この磐座は現在も真幣明神（みてぐらみょうじん）として祀られている。 元々は真幣明神（美濃国神名帳）という名の神社でという。[[壬申の乱]]で功績を挙げた[[村国氏]]・各務氏、また[[承久の乱]]以降守護家として[[土岐氏]]、また土岐氏の家来にあった薄田（すすきだ）氏、佐良木（更木）氏と時代の中で諸豪族に崇敬と手厚い保護を受ける。 [[永禄]]10年（[[1567年]]）、[[織田信長]]は当社で[[岐阜城|稲葉山城]]攻めの戦勝祈願を行い、戦勝後は広大な社領を寄進したという。寺社勢力にも容赦ない攻撃で臨んだ信長にしては珍しいが、これは信長が各務原へ攻め入り、周囲の寺を焼き払って当社に迫ったところ、霧が立ち込め体の自由が利かなくなり落馬したことによる。信長はこれを神罰と恐れ、以後崇敬し各務野原近里（現在の那加地区・尾崎団地・[[岐阜基地|航空自衛隊岐阜基地]]・市役所一帯）千三百[[町歩]]を社領に付し、宝物等を寄進し文化財として今日に至る。 その後も旗本[[坪内氏|坪内]]・徳山等諸氏の崇敬も深く、数百年来八ヶ村氏子崇敬者により神事・祭礼・造営修復事業が変わりなく奉仕される。[[1879年]]（明治12年）に郷社となる 。現在は那加地区（那加、尾崎）総社として崇敬されている。信長が戦勝祈願したことから、今日では、勝運・開運の神としてスポーツ関係等各種団体の勝運・必勝祈願の参拝者で賑わう。 == 祭神 == === 主祭神 === * [[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] === 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高御産霊神]] : 美濃国神名帳に記載の牟須比女神に該当 * [[カミムスビ|神産霊神]] * 美和都神 : 美濃国神名帳に記載の美和都明神に該当 * 宇津井剣火御子三所神 : 美濃国神名帳に記載の宇津井劔火御子明神に該当 * [[伊波乃西神社|伊波乃西神]] * 長山神 * [[村国神社|村国神]] * [[村国真墨田神社|村国真墨田神]] == 境内社 == * 秋葉神社（[[カグツチ|秋葉大神]]） - [[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）建立美濃国那加総社手力雄神社参拝の栞。 * 八幡神社（[[八幡神|八幡大神]]） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立。[[石清水八幡宮]]より勧請。 * 福徳龍神社（[[龍神]]） * 稲荷神社（[[稲荷神|稲荷大神]]） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立。[[伏見稲荷大社]]より勧請。 * 子護薬師（[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナヒコナ|少彦名命]]・薬師大神） - [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）創建 * 高徳福龍神社（龍神） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立 == 主な神事 == * 1月1日 - 歳旦祭 * 1月2日以降 - 新年開運・交通安全祈祷 * 1月第二日曜日 - 秋葉神社例祭・[[左義長]] * 2月第一日曜日 - 厄除祈願祭 * 2月11日 - 紀元祭 * 2月17日 - 祈年祭 * 2月23日 - 天長祭 * 3月第三日曜日 - 稲荷神社例祭・厄除[[餅撒き]] * 4月28日・29日 - 例大祭 * 6月30日 - 夏越大祓式 * 8月第二土曜日 - 薬師祭（雅楽奉納・献灯祭） * 8月第二日曜日 - 高徳福龍神例祭 * 9月23日 - 八幡神社例祭 * 10月～12月 - 七五三祭 * 11月23日 - 新嘗祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式・除夜祭 * 毎月1日、15日 - 月次祭　 == 文化財 == === 県指定文化財 === ==== 彫刻 ==== * 狛犬{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=県指定文化財 |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=各務原市}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=狛犬［こまいぬ］（手力雄神社） |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=岐阜県（文化伝承課）}} === 市指定文化財{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=市指定文化財 |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=各務原市}} === ==== 史跡 ==== * 手力雄神社境内古墳 : [[古墳時代]]後期の横穴式石室墳。東古墳と西古墳の2つがある。 ==== 建造物 ==== *手力雄神社御本殿 : [[延宝]]二年（[[1674年]]）再建。 ==== 彫刻 ==== * 竜の雌雄 ==== 工芸 ==== * 手力雄神社獅子頭 ==== 書跡 ==== * 織田信長禁制 ==== 天然記念物 ==== * 信長公弓掛桜・的場桜 === その他 === * 磐座 == 名称 == この神社は「てじからおじんじゃ」「てぢからおじんじゃ」「たぢからおじんじゃ」などと一般には呼ばれているが、神社の在る各務原市の神社を統括している岐阜県神社庁各務原市支部が毎年1月初頭に発行する「新春 各務原市金幣社 五社巡り スタンプラリー」の台紙には、「手力雄」の部分に「てぢからおお」ときちんと記されている[https://ameblo.jp/yachi3749/entry-12343770119.html 個人のブログ]。金幣社五社巡りの中で手力雄神社に絞られた記事。。 == 逸話 == 鎮座地は各務原台地最西の水のつかない場所で、すぐ脇には[[木曽川]]（旧広野河）が流れ、水に関わりの深い地域である。本殿の軒に4m近い龍の彫刻が巻きついている。昔話で夜、田畑を荒らし昼には井戸に隠れていた。龍の悪さに恐れて龍の目に釘を打ちつけたら田畑が荒らされなくなったと伝わる。もとより、山々を見渡せる岐阜周辺には山岳信仰があり東山道には奥社に天手力雄命を祀る長野県鎮座[[戸隠神社]]、九頭龍社がある。九頭龍社は天手力雄命が奉斎される以前に地主神として奉斎されており、古い時代からの龍神信仰も見られる。 == 手力雄神社（岐阜市）と手力雄神社（各務原市）の関係 == 岐阜市の手力雄神社は、[[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）創建とされるが、[[各務郡]]と[[厚見郡]]の有力者、各務吉雄と吉宗の手により各務郡の各務氏の氏神（各務原市の手力雄神社）を厚見郡に分祀したといわれている。別の説では、朝廷の宮中の祭神を分祀したものであり、元々の祭神は[[伊勢神宮]]の天手力雄神であるという。 各務原市の手力雄神社は「手力」の初見が永禄10年（[[1567年]]）のことで信長公が寄進した宝物に記載されるとのことで、その前には「長塚宮」文明7年（[[1475年]]）とある（これは拝殿北側の箱棟（[[1682年]]）には卍型が残っており神仏習合の名残りがある為混同したのではないか。）。古くは[[938年]]から[[956年]]に編纂された美濃国神名帳では「真幣明神」他、現在の相殿神の神名も見られ、古墳時代の後期（[[6世紀]]後半）の古墳も存在することから古い時代より神聖な場所として信仰があったことが窺い知れる。 岐阜市下川手には各務吉雄が祀ったと伝わる[[石切神社 (岐阜市)|石切神社]]がある。こちらは各務氏の祖神（鏡作りの祖）石凝姥命を祀っているという。各務氏が天の岩戸に関わる神を佐良木郷八ヶ村山中に祀った（[[938年]] - [[1567年]]の間、実際には各務氏が勢力を持っていた頃なので前半頃）とすれば自然である。 岐阜市の手力雄神社と各務原市の手力雄神社は近いこともあり、混同することがある。そのため、それぞれの神社では混同しないように呼びかけている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tezikarao.org/sp/news/detail.php?id=229|title=●注意●当社は火祭りを行う神社ではありません。|access-date=2024-10-27 |publisher=手力雄神社（各務原市）}}。 == 交通アクセス == ; 路線バス * [[各務原市ふれあいバス]]那加線 ** 「山後町公民館前」バス停より徒歩で約3分。 ; 鉄道 * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]] [[新加納駅]]から北へ1km。 ; 自動車 * いちょう通り（各務原市の[[道路愛称]]）を西進し、右手に鳥居が見える。鳥居手前で右折すると境内内側の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} ; 各務原市の金弊社 * [[加佐美神社]] * [[村国神社]] * [[村国真墨田神社]] * [[御井神社 (各務原市)|御井神社]] ; 関連 * [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] * [[手力雄神社 (大垣市)]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tezikarao.org/ 那加総社 手力雄神社｜岐阜県各務原市]公式ウェブサイト * [https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011619/1005237.html 織田信長とのつながりを示す文書] - [[各務原市]]公式ウェブサイト * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/unumajukujuunin/mukasihanasi/tetikarao.htm |title=あの信長がマイッタ手力雄神社 |date=20190330043923}}　信長が当社を崇敬するようになったいきさつなど。 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしや かかみかはら}} [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] to [[Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

いちょう通りから見た様子 | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[各務原市]][[那加手力町]]4 | 位置={{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]]
[[金幣社]] | 創建=[[6世紀]] | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[4月28日]]・[[4月29日|29日]] | 神事= | 画像説明=いちょう通りから見た様子 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=手力雄神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''手力雄神社'''（てぢからおおじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[各務原市]]那加にある[[神社]]。各務原市[[那加町|那加地区]]の[[産土神]]（那加総社）で、各務原市内[[金幣社|金幣五社]]の一社である。 == 歴史 == [[6世紀]]末期ごろ中里（現那加地区）を支配していた豪族により、佐良木郷八ケ村（西市場、新加納、前野、北洞、岩地、長塚、桐野、山後）の山の中腹に[[磐座]]を祀ったのが始まり。この磐座は現在も真幣明神（みてぐらみょうじん）として祀られている。 元々は真幣明神（美濃国神名帳）という名の神社でという。[[壬申の乱]]で功績を挙げた[[村国氏]]・各務氏、また[[承久の乱]]以降守護家として[[土岐氏]]、また土岐氏の家来にあった薄田（すすきだ）氏、佐良木（更木）氏と時代の中で諸豪族に崇敬と手厚い保護を受ける。 [[永禄]]10年（[[1567年]]）、[[織田信長]]は当社で[[岐阜城|稲葉山城]]攻めの戦勝祈願を行い、戦勝後は広大な社領を寄進したという。寺社勢力にも容赦ない攻撃で臨んだ信長にしては珍しいが、これは信長が各務原へ攻め入り、周囲の寺を焼き払って当社に迫ったところ、霧が立ち込め体の自由が利かなくなり落馬したことによる。信長はこれを神罰と恐れ、以後崇敬し各務野原近里（現在の那加地区・尾崎団地・[[岐阜基地|航空自衛隊岐阜基地]]・市役所一帯）千三百[[町歩]]を社領に付し、宝物等を寄進し文化財として今日に至る。 その後も旗本[[坪内氏|坪内]]・徳山等諸氏の崇敬も深く、数百年来八ヶ村氏子崇敬者により神事・祭礼・造営修復事業が変わりなく奉仕される。[[1879年]]（明治12年）に郷社となる 。現在は那加地区（那加、尾崎）総社として崇敬されている。信長が戦勝祈願したことから、今日では、勝運・開運の神としてスポーツ関係等各種団体の勝運・必勝祈願の参拝者で賑わう。 == 祭神 == === 主祭神 === * [[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力雄神]] === 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高御産霊神]] : 美濃国神名帳に記載の牟須比女神に該当 * [[カミムスビ|神産霊神]] * 美和都神 : 美濃国神名帳に記載の美和都明神に該当 * 宇津井剣火御子三所神 : 美濃国神名帳に記載の宇津井劔火御子明神に該当 * [[伊波乃西神社|伊波乃西神]] * 長山神 * [[村国神社|村国神]] * [[村国真墨田神社|村国真墨田神]] == 境内社 == * 秋葉神社（[[カグツチ|秋葉大神]]） - [[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）建立美濃国那加総社手力雄神社参拝の栞。 * 八幡神社（[[八幡神|八幡大神]]） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立。[[石清水八幡宮]]より勧請。 * 福徳龍神社（[[龍神]]） * 稲荷神社（[[稲荷神|稲荷大神]]） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立。[[伏見稲荷大社]]より勧請。 * 子護薬師（[[大国主|大巳貴命]]・[[スクナヒコナ|少彦名命]]・薬師大神） - [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）創建 * 高徳福龍神社（龍神） - 延宝2年（1674年）建立 == 主な神事 == * 1月1日 - 歳旦祭 * 1月2日以降 - 新年開運・交通安全祈祷 * 1月第二日曜日 - 秋葉神社例祭・[[左義長]] * 2月第一日曜日 - 厄除祈願祭 * 2月11日 - 紀元祭 * 2月17日 - 祈年祭 * 2月23日 - 天長祭 * 3月第三日曜日 - 稲荷神社例祭・厄除[[餅撒き]] * 4月28日・29日 - 例大祭 * 6月30日 - 夏越大祓式 * 8月第二土曜日 - 薬師祭（雅楽奉納・献灯祭） * 8月第二日曜日 - 高徳福龍神例祭 * 9月23日 - 八幡神社例祭 * 10月～12月 - 七五三祭 * 11月23日 - 新嘗祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式・除夜祭 * 毎月1日、15日 - 月次祭　 == 文化財 == === 県指定文化財 === ==== 彫刻 ==== * 狛犬{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=県指定文化財 |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=各務原市}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=狛犬［こまいぬ］（手力雄神社） |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=岐阜県（文化伝承課）}} === 市指定文化財{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=市指定文化財 |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=各務原市}} === ==== 史跡 ==== * 手力雄神社境内古墳 : [[古墳時代]]後期の横穴式石室墳。東古墳と西古墳の2つがある。 ==== 建造物 ==== *手力雄神社御本殿 : [[延宝]]二年（[[1674年]]）再建。 ==== 彫刻 ==== * 竜の雌雄 ==== 工芸 ==== * 手力雄神社獅子頭 ==== 書跡 ==== * 織田信長禁制 ==== 天然記念物 ==== * 信長公弓掛桜・的場桜 === その他 === * 磐座 == 名称 == この神社は「てじからおじんじゃ」「てぢからおじんじゃ」「たぢからおじんじゃ」などと一般には呼ばれているが、神社の在る各務原市の神社を統括している岐阜県神社庁各務原市支部が毎年1月初頭に発行する「新春 各務原市金幣社 五社巡り スタンプラリー」の台紙には、「手力雄」の部分に「てぢからおお」ときちんと記されている[https://ameblo.jp/yachi3749/entry-12343770119.html 個人のブログ]。金幣社五社巡りの中で手力雄神社に絞られた記事。。 == 逸話 == 鎮座地は各務原台地最西の水のつかない場所で、すぐ脇には[[木曽川]]（旧広野河）が流れ、水に関わりの深い地域である。本殿の軒に4m近い龍の彫刻が巻きついている。昔話で夜、田畑を荒らし昼には井戸に隠れていた。龍の悪さに恐れて龍の目に釘を打ちつけたら田畑が荒らされなくなったと伝わる。もとより、山々を見渡せる岐阜周辺には山岳信仰があり東山道には奥社に天手力雄命を祀る長野県鎮座[[戸隠神社]]、九頭龍社がある。九頭龍社は天手力雄命が奉斎される以前に地主神として奉斎されており、古い時代からの龍神信仰も見られる。 == 手力雄神社（岐阜市）と手力雄神社（各務原市）の関係 == 岐阜市の手力雄神社は、[[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）創建とされるが、[[各務郡]]と[[厚見郡]]の有力者、各務吉雄と吉宗の手により各務郡の各務氏の氏神（各務原市の手力雄神社）を厚見郡に分祀したといわれている。別の説では、朝廷の宮中の祭神を分祀したものであり、元々の祭神は[[伊勢神宮]]の天手力雄神であるという。 各務原市の手力雄神社は「手力」の初見が永禄10年（[[1567年]]）のことで信長公が寄進した宝物に記載されるとのことで、その前には「長塚宮」文明7年（[[1475年]]）とある（これは拝殿北側の箱棟（[[1682年]]）には卍型が残っており神仏習合の名残りがある為混同したのではないか。）。古くは[[938年]]から[[956年]]に編纂された美濃国神名帳では「真幣明神」他、現在の相殿神の神名も見られ、古墳時代の後期（[[6世紀]]後半）の古墳も存在することから古い時代より神聖な場所として信仰があったことが窺い知れる。 岐阜市下川手には各務吉雄が祀ったと伝わる[[石切神社 (岐阜市)|石切神社]]がある。こちらは各務氏の祖神（鏡作りの祖）石凝姥命を祀っているという。各務氏が天の岩戸に関わる神を佐良木郷八ヶ村山中に祀った（[[938年]] - [[1567年]]の間、実際には各務氏が勢力を持っていた頃なので前半頃）とすれば自然である。 岐阜市の手力雄神社と各務原市の手力雄神社は近いこともあり、混同することがある。そのため、それぞれの神社では混同しないように呼びかけている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tezikarao.org/sp/news/detail.php?id=229|title=●注意●当社は火祭りを行う神社ではありません。|access-date=2024-10-27 |publisher=手力雄神社（各務原市）}}。 == 交通アクセス == ; 路線バス * [[各務原市ふれあいバス]]那加線 ** 「山後町公民館前」バス停より徒歩で約3分。 ; 鉄道 * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄各務原線|各務原線]] [[新加納駅]]から北へ1km。 ; 自動車 * いちょう通り（各務原市の[[道路愛称]]）を西進し、右手に鳥居が見える。鳥居手前で右折すると境内内側の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} ; 各務原市の金弊社 * [[加佐美神社]] * [[村国神社]] * [[村国真墨田神社]] * [[御井神社 (各務原市)|御井神社]] ; 関連 * [[手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] * [[手力雄神社 (大垣市)]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tezikarao.org/ 那加総社 手力雄神社｜岐阜県各務原市]公式ウェブサイト * [https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011619/1005237.html 織田信長とのつながりを示す文書] - [[各務原市]]公式ウェブサイト * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/unumajukujuunin/mukasihanasi/tetikarao.htm |title=あの信長がマイッタ手力雄神社 |date=20190330043923}}　信長が当社を崇敬するようになったいきさつなど。 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしや かかみかはら}} [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|ja|土岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Japan)|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō|ja|左義長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|ja|土岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Japan)|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō|ja|左義長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakamigahara|qq=}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|qq=}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|qq=}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}}・{{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 29|qq=}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|qq=}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakamigahara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|qq=}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakura|qq=}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|qq=}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|qq=}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|qq=}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|ja|土岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki clan|qq=}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyū War|qq=}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku|qq=}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|qq=}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Japan)|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōō (Japan)|qq=}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō|ja|左義長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō|qq=}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Kakamigahara history]] [[Category:Kakamigahara buildings]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakamigahara|qq=}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|qq=}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|qq=}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|ja|4月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 28|qq=}}・{{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 29|qq=}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|qq=}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakamigahara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|qq=}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakura|qq=}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|qq=}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|qq=}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|qq=}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|ja|土岐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki clan|qq=}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyū War|qq=}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku|qq=}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|qq=}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Japan)|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōō (Japan)|qq=}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō|ja|左義長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō|qq=}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|qq=}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|qq=}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|qq=}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|qq=}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|qq=}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|qq=}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|qq=}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|qq=}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:各務原市の神社]]

Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|qq=}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|qq=}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|qq=}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|qq=}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|qq=}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|qq=}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|qq=}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|qq=}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Kinpei shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)]] [[ja:%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%90%84%E5%8B%99%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|qq=}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|qq=}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|qq=}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in
