Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. [[sn:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11496923]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) [[sn:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11496923]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}}

Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}}

[[commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11496923]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. [[co:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496923]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]]{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:April 28|April 28]]・[[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kakamigahara|Kakamigahara]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ([[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura]]) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the [[:en:Toki clan|Toki clan]] after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]]. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1567), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao]] === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō (Japan)]] (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagichō]] * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) [[co:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)]][[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11496923]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Tejikarao Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tejikarao-jinja kakamigahara2008-1.jpg|270px]]
View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|en|Kakamigahara|WD=Q641491|lt=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|en|April 28|WD=Q2534|lt=April 28}}・{{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|en|Kakamigahara|WD=Q641491|lt=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|WD=Q687635|lt=Toki clan}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Jōō (Japan)}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手力雄神社]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|WD=Q687635|lt=Toki clan}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Jōō (Japan)}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手力雄神社]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

View from Ichō-dōri | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Kakamigahara|en|Kakamigahara|WD=Q641491|lt=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|Nakatejikara-chō|ja|那加手力町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara-chō|1=Nakatejikara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131870048}} 4 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ameno-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} | type = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | consecration_year = {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival = {{ill|April 28|en|April 28|WD=Q2534|lt=April 28}}・{{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} | Description = View from Ichō-dōri | Map = {{location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=24|lat_sec=31.2|lon_deg=136|lon_min=49|lon_sec=19.1|label=Tejikarao Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|31.28|N|136|49|19.15|E}}}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (手力雄神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Nakatejikara district|ja|那加地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatejikara district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kakamigahara|en|Kakamigahara|WD=Q641491|lt=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (regional tutelary deity) of the Nakatejikara area and is one of the {{ill|Five Kinpei Shrines|ja|金幣五社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinpei Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Kakamigahara. == History == The shrine's origins trace back to the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, when a powerful local clan in the Nakatejikara area enshrined a sacred rock ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura}}) on the hillside. This sacred rock, which remains enshrined as {{ill|Mitegura Myōjin|ja|真幣明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitegura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marked the foundation of the shrine. Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|WD=Q687635|lt=Toki clan}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Jōō (Japan)}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手力雄神社]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Originally named Mitegura Myōjin (as noted in the {{ill|Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province Shinmeichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} due to the support of the {{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakami clan|ja|各務氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakami clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and later the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|WD=Q687635|lt=Toki clan}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}. These clans revered and protected the shrine. In the 10th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1567), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} prayed at this shrine for victory during his attack on {{ill|Mount Inaba Castle|ja|稲葉山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Inaba Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜城|1=Gifu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1367052}}. Following his success, Nobunaga dedicated extensive lands and treasures to the shrine, a rare act of reverence considering his reputation for suppressing religious powers. The act was inspired by an incident where Nobunaga's army was hindered by a mysterious fog near the shrine, which he took as divine intervention. Over the centuries, the shrine continued to be revered by prominent families such as the {{ill|Tsubouchi clan|ja|坪内氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubouchi clan|1=Tsubouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425737}} and Tokuyama. Its role as a central shrine for the Nakatejikara area has remained strong, attracting visitors for prayers, especially for victories and good fortune. == Enshrined Deities == === Main deity === * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} === Additional Enshrined Deities === * [[Takamimusubi]] * [[Kamimusubi]] * Miwato-no-Kami * Utsui Kenka Mikomizo-no-Kami * {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}} * Nagayama-no-Kami * {{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Masumida Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Akiba Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Fukutoku Ryūjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kogori Yakushi : consecration_year in the 2nd year of {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|WD=Q1145081|lt=Jōō (Japan)}} (1653) * Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine == festivals and Events == * January 1 - Saitan-sai * January 2 onward - New Year prayers * Closest Sunday to January 15 - Akiba Shrine Festival and {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō}} * First Sunday of February - Yakuyoke Kigan-sai * February 11 - Kigen-sai * February 17 - Kinen-sai * Third Sunday of March - Inari Shrine Festival and Yakuyoke Mochi Throwing * April 28 & 29 - Main Festival * June 30 - Nagoshi Ōharae-shiki * Second Saturday of August - Yakushi Festival (Gagaku and Lantern Festival) * Second Sunday of August - Kōtoku Fukuryū Shrine Festival * September 23 - Hachiman Shrine Festival * November - Shichi-go-san Prayers * November 23 - Niiname-sai * December 23 - Tennō-sai * December 31 - Ōharae-shiki and Joya-sai * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai == Cultural Properties == === Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sculptures ==== * Komainu (Lion-Dog Statues){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005179.html |title=Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12505.html |title=Komainu (Tejikarao Shrine) |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Gifu Prefecture (Cultural Transmission Division)}} === City-Designated Cultural Properties{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-11-12 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}} === ==== Historic Sites ==== * Ancient Kofuns on the Shrine Grounds : Late Kofun period stone chamber tombs, consisting of East and West Kofuns. ==== Structures ==== * Tejikarao Shrine Main Hall : Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} (1674) ==== Sculptures ==== * Male and Female Dragons ==== Craftworks ==== * Lion Head of Tejikarao Shrine ==== Writings ==== * Oda Nobunaga’s Prohibition Order ==== Natural Monuments ==== * Sakura Trees: Nobunaga’s Yumikake Sakura and Matoba Sakura === Other === * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) ==References== {{translated page|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|version=104132438|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手力雄神社]] {{Commons category|Tejikarao-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} [[ja:手力雄神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11496922}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
